objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 password-reset privacy assessment with exposed reset tokens in support audit logs.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Users, reset tokens, and support audit events are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map password-reset and support audit surfaces.
actions: Request a synthetic password reset for a staff account
Inspect whether support audit logs are exposed without authorization
successCriteria: Reset request behavior and exposed audit-log evidence are recorded
noSubagents: account-takeover impact scoping stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate reset-token reuse from exposed logs.
actions: Extract the synthetic reset token from the exposed audit log
Use the token to complete a reset without mailbox or help-desk verification
Record the missing token redaction and reset confirmation controls
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites reset request, exposed log, and token-use evidence
noSubagents: final severity and disclosure wording stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic password reset request response
evidence.summary: A password-reset request for a synthetic staff account returned a generic success response while creating a reset token in the audit stream.

evidence.title: Synthetic exposed reset token audit log
evidence.summary: An unauthenticated support audit-log endpoint returned the active password-reset token in clear text.

evidence.title: Synthetic leaked reset token password change
evidence.summary: The leaked reset token changed the synthetic staff account password without additional mailbox, staff, or help-desk verification.

findings.title: Password reset tokens leak through support audit logs
findings.description: The synthetic support audit-log endpoint exposes active password-reset tokens and the reset completion endpoint accepts those tokens without an additional verification step.
findings.impact: An attacker who can reach the audit-log endpoint could reset a staff account and gain access to student, classroom, or administrative data protected by that account.
findings.remediation: Remove secrets from audit responses, require authorization for support logs, store only token hashes, expire tokens quickly, and require mailbox or help-desk verification before completing high-risk resets.
